label: Low-risk chest pain after negative ED workup
inclusion_criteria: Chest pain evaluated in ED and judged low risk for discharge.
No active ischemia, unstable vital signs, or admission need.
exclusion_criteria: Abnormal ECG or troponin requiring admission.
Pulmonary embolism, aortic emergency, myocarditis, or pericarditis concern.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care or cardiology as instructed.

source_audit.notes: AHA supports chest pain warning-sign education, but not static low-risk ED risk stratification. This draft is retired from ontology expansion and should remain product-layer fallback unless later rebuilt as a narrower reviewed phenotype.

review.notes: Phase 22 retired this broad static ontology draft. Chest pain risk stratification depends on ECG, troponin, protocol, local follow-up, and clinician judgment; use product-layer fallback unless rebuilt as a narrower source-supported phenotype.
